title: Xmas Day In Ye Morning
content:
1.
    Dame, get up and bake your pies,
    Bake your pies, bake your pies;
    Dame, get up and bake your pies,
    On Christmas-day in the morning.

2.
    Dame, what makes your maidens lie,
    Maidens lie, maidens lie?
    Dame, what makes your maidens lie,
    On Christmas-day in the morning?

3.
    Dame, what makes your ducks to die,
    Ducks to die, ducks to die?
    Dame, what makes your ducks to die,
    On Christmas-day in the morning?

4.
    Their wings are cut, they cannot fly,
    Cannot fly, cannot fly;
    Their wings are cut, they cannot fly,
    On Christmas-day in the morning.
meaning:
"Xmas Day In Ye Morning" is a quintessential example of Walter Crane's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
